reasoning: The Lean statement directly encodes "H is generated by H \ {1}" as `Subgroup.closure ((H : Set G) \ {1}) = H`. The coercion `(H : Set G)` gives the underlying set of the subgroup, `\ {1}` is set difference removing the identity, and `Subgroup.closure` is the subgroup generated by a set (defined in Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Lattice.lean as `sInf { K | k ⊆ K }`). The equality with H captures the "is generated by" claim. No hypotheses are added or dropped, no types are altered, and the edge case H = ⊥ also works (H \ {1} = ∅, closure ∅ = ⊥ = H). The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal statement.
